top_signals.title: Iran Was Never Going To Do a Deal Says Nikki Haley
top_signals.summary: Nikki Haley, Trump's ambassador to the UN in his first term, joined Balance of Power, discuss the latest in the Middle East. She said the Trump should stop negotiating with Iran and urged him to "go finish what you started." (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Consumer prices rose 4.2% annually in May, highest in three years
top_signals.summary: The consumer price index was expected to show a 4.2% gain from a year ago, according to the Dow Jones consensus estimate.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Bitcoin Selloff Leaves Half of All Supply Trading at a Loss
top_signals.summary: Bitcoin’s latest retreat has been so severe, it has pushed more than half its circulating supply into loss-making territory.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Mike Ashley’s Frasers makes £1.7bn takeover offer for Hugo Boss
top_signals.summary: Mike Ashley’s Frasers Group has moved to tighten its grip on German fashion giant Hugo Boss, tabling a takeover offer in a deal that values the fashion brand at nearly €2bn. The retail empire, which spans Sports Direct and Flannels, already sits as Hugo Boss’s largest investor with a stake north of 25 per cent, [...]

top_signals.summary: Oracle PeopleSoft servers are being targeted in ongoing data theft attacks by the ShinyHunters extortion gang, which claims to have stolen data from over 100 organizations. [...]

top_signals.title: New Foreign Direct Investment in the United States, 2025
top_signals.summary: Expenditures by foreign direct investors to acquire, establish, or expand U.S. businesses totaled $232.2 billion in 2025, according to preliminary statistics released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. Expenditures increased $76.8 billion, or 49.5 percent, from 2024 levels. As in previous years, acquisitions of existing U.S. businesses accounted for most of the expenditures. Acquisition expenditures in 2025 were $218.4 billion, expenditures to establish new U.S. businesses were $4.6 billion, and expenditures to expand existing foreign-owned businesses were $9.2 billion. Planned total expenditures, which include both first-year and planned future expenditures, were $284.5 billion. Full Text
